docs.title: GPS Code Collection
docs.text:
The GPS Code Collection provides software for the analysis of GPS radio occultation observations.

This MkDocs manual is the lightweight user-facing entry point for the repository. It complements:

  • the top-level README.md for installation notes
  • the generated Doxygen reference for implementation details
  • the regression tests in tests/ for concrete workflow examples

docs.title: What You Will Find Here
docs.text:
• a quick build and test guide
  • an overview of the available command-line tools
  • links to the main project resources

docs.title: Main Components
docs.text:
The repository is organized around a C-based command-line toolkit in src/, together with:

  • local dependency builds in libs/
  • regression tests in tests/
  • documentation sources in docs/

The source tree builds several executables for working with profile data, maps, perturbations, tropopause diagnostics, and related statistics.

docs.title: External Components
docs.text: The files src/jurassic.c and src/jurassic.h are included here for convenience from the external JURASSIC radiative transfer model.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
We are interested in sharing the GPS Code Collection for research applications. For questions or support, please contact:

Dr. Lars Hoffmann Jülich Supercomputing Centre, Forschungszentrum Jülich l.hoffmann@fz-juelich.de

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This page summarizes the usual setup, build, and test workflow for the GPS Code Collection on Linux.

docs.title: Repository Layout
docs.text:
The repository is organized into a few main areas:

  • src/: C source files, headers, and the main Makefile
  • tests/: regression tests and reference outputs
  • libs/: vendored dependency archives and the local build helper
  • docs/: Doxygen and MkDocs documentation sources

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
You need a standard C toolchain, including:

  • gcc
  • make

The code also depends on:

  • GNU Scientific Library (GSL)
  • netCDF C library

If those libraries are not already available on your system, the repository provides local copies and a helper build script.

docs.title: Build Dependencies
docs.text:
To build the vendored libraries locally:

cd libs
./build.sh

The default source build looks for headers and libraries in:

  • ../libs/build/include
  • ../libs/build/lib
  • ../libs/build/lib64

docs.title: Build The Code
docs.text:
Compile the executables from src/:

cd src
make

Useful build options from the Makefile:

  • STATIC=1: enable static linking
  • OPT=-O0 or OPT=-O3: change optimization level
  • INFO=1: emit optimization information
  • PROF=1: build with profiling flags
  • COV=1: build with coverage instrumentation

Example:

cd src
make OPT=-O0 COV=1

docs.title: Run Regression Tests
docs.text:
The repository includes shell-based regression tests:

cd src
make check

This runs the current test suite:

  • pert_test
  • tropo_test

You can also run a single test directly:

cd tests/pert_test
./run.sh

The test scripts create temporary output in a local data/ directory and compare it with checked-in reference results under data.ref/.

docs.title: Install Executables
docs.text:
The default install target copies executables to ../bin:

cd src
make install

To override the target directory:

cd src
make DESTDIR=/path/to/bin install

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The build uses strict compiler warnings and treats warnings as errors via -Werror.
  • Static linking may require adjustments on some systems; if it causes issues, review the STATIC setting and linker flags in src/Makefile.
  • The files src/jurassic.c and src/jurassic.h are vendored from the external JURASSIC radiative transfer model.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
These links provide further information for users of the GPS Code Collection:

  • Project repository
  • README file
  • MkDocs user manual
  • Doxygen manual
  • JURASSIC upstream repository
  • AI documentation
  • Citation file
  • License

docs.title: What They Are Used For
docs.text:
The tools cover a small set of common workflows around GPS radio occultation data:

  • date and time conversion utilities
  • event extraction and tabular summaries
  • map and profile export
  • perturbation analysis
  • tropopause diagnostics
  • response and variance calculations

docs.title: Related Components
docs.text:
Several executables share infrastructure from:

  • libgps.c and libgps.h: project-specific I/O and analysis helpers
  • jurassic.c and jurassic.h: vendored code from the external JURASSIC radiative transfer model

docs.title: Build And Test Entry Points
docs.text:
Most users will interact with the tools through the main build workflow:

cd src
make
make check

For implementation details and APIs, see the Doxygen manual linked from the documentation site.
